original_abstract: This chapter explores the intersection of language, identity, and power within contemporary social frameworks. By examining the nuances of linguistic performance, the author illuminates how individuals navigate complex cultural landscapes to assert agency and negotiate social standing. Drawing upon a diverse range of interdisciplinary theories, the study provides a critical analysis of how discourse shapes—and is shaped by—shifting global dynamics. Ultimately, the work offers new insights into the evolving relationship between communication and the construction of the self in an increasingly interconnected world.
humanized_abstract: Language, identity and power are the key concepts explored in this chapter. The author examines language use as a performance in social life and discusses how individuals can exercise power and gain status in a highly complex and potentially fragmented social world. The study draws on a wide range of theoretical perspectives from across the disciplines. A critical exploration of the ways in which discourse relates to changing patterns of globalisation, this work advances our understanding of the complex and dynamic relationship between communication and identity.
